page: UpCloud DEV-1xCPU-1GB-10GB is an entry-level virtual server belonging to the Developer family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single shared vCPU powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 1.0 GB of system memory and 10 GB of local SSD storage, along with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this server is designed for lightweight tasks. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests both scored 753.39, placing the instance in the bottom 10% of benchmarked servers. Memory bandwidth is recorded at 104.39 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 7.54 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 8.68 GB/sec for large-block writes. The PassMark CPU score is 2011.65, and the PassMark CPU encryption test reached 1129.48, reflecting a weak to poor performance profile across standard compute metrics.

Due to the shared CPU allocation and limited 1.0 GB memory capacity, this server exhibits significant resource constraints and is not suitable for production databases or high-throughput applications. However, it offers a highly cost-efficient profile for low-demand environments. It is best suited for software development, testing, lightweight scripting, hosting static web pages with low traffic, or serving as a basic sandbox environment.
description: The UpCloud DEV-1xCPU-1GB-10GB is an entry-level virtual server from the Developer family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with a single shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPU clocked at 2.0 GHz, 1.0 GB of RAM, and 10 GB of local SSD storage. Designed for budget-conscious and low-resource scenarios, its performance benchmarks reflect its entry-level positioning, with stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores falling into the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth ranges from 104.39 GB/sec for cached reads to 7.54 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Given its shared CPU allocation and minimal memory footprint, this instance is highly cost-efficient for non-production workloads. It is best suited for software development, basic testing, running lightweight scripts, or hosting low-traffic static websites.
